frameworks.name: individual-differences statistical learning
frameworks.one_line: treats SL as a domain-general ability sortable from bad to good learners

frameworks.name: domain-tuned statistical learning
frameworks.one_line: SL computations tuned to statistics of real-world sensory inputs within domains

frameworks.name: cognitive (dis)ability correlational framework
frameworks.one_line: ties SL variability to broader cognitive abilities and disabilities

core_question: Is there a general statistical learning capacity that ranks individuals from bad to good across cognitive domains?
key_assumptions: the individual-differences view assumes SL is a unitary general ability
performance across domains should covary if SL is general
real-world sensory inputs have domain-specific statistical structure
key_findings: current evidence supporting a general SL capacity is meager
SL variability is better understood relative to domain-specific statistical environments
interpretations: SL should be reconceived as learning tuned to domain-specific real-world input statistics
this reframing implies a radically different agenda for SL research
